docs.text: The meuh programming language Introduction The goal of this project is to implement a programming language which can permit any programmer to work easier without ruminating bad injury about used language. On the second hand, this can be a hobby for a time. Kind of language The language adopts the philosophy of write once, compile anywhere and KISS . It is a general purpose Turing Complete language with a least those paradigms : Imperative Object-Oriented Procedural Concurrent Generic Programming Reflection The next features will also be implemented : Thread multiplexing with LLVM coroutines like in go with the goroutine Anonymous functions called as Lambda expressions ... to be continued Source code example package io.meuh.test; import meuh.lang.Number; import meuh.lang.Integer; public class IntegerCalculator extends Calculator implement PolynomialResolver { public int main(const String[] &argv) throws PolynomialException { if (argv.length < 1) { System.stdout.println("Need arguments"); return 0; } return resolve(new Number[]{ Integer.decodeString(argv[1]), Integer.decodeString(argv[2]), }).toInt(); } protected Number resolve(const Number[] &numbers) throws PolynomialException { if (numbers.length != 2) { throw new IllegalArgumentException("Only 2 numbers are expected, not: " + numbers.length); } return resolve(numbers[0], numbers[1]); } /** Solve a*x+b=0 */ public Integer polynome(const Integer &a, const Integer &b) throws PolynomialException { if (a == 0) { throw new PolynomialException("The value of a is zero"); } return -b/a; } } The toolchain The compiler front-end will be part of this project, and LLVM will be used for the back-end . Compilation phases per compilation unit : Lexical Analyzer : Produce tokens. Syntax Analyzer : Produce an Abstract Syntax Tree by the usage of an hand written Recursive Descent Parser . Semantic Analyzer : Check languages rules. SSA Converter : Convert the AST tree into a SSA tree. LLVMifier : Convert the SSA tree into LLVM assembly language. llvm-as : Translate LLVM assembly language into LLVM bitcode. llvm-link : Link all compilation units together. opt : Run global optimization llc : Compile optimized LLVM bytecode into a native object file lld : Use the LLVM linker to link the native object file into an executable binary. 1 Optionally, the 9th and 10th step can be replaced with lli to directly execute program from LLVM bitcode. License Copyright © 2019-2021 VERDOÏA Laurent <verdoialaurent@gmail.com> This program is free software: you can redistribute it and/or modify it under the terms of the GNU General Public License as published by the Free Software Foundation, either version 3 of the License, or (at your option) any later version. This program is distributed in the hope that it will be useful, but WITHOUT ANY WARRANTY; without even the implied warranty of MERCHANTABILITY or FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE. See the GNU General Public License for more details. You should have received a copy of the GNU General Public License along with this program. If not, see <https://www.gnu.org/licenses/>. On macOS, only the host linker is supported ↩

docs.text: package io.meuh.test; import meuh.lang.Number; import meuh.lang.Integer; public class IntegerCalculator extends Calculator implement PolynomialResolver { public int main(const String[] &argv) throws PolynomialException { if (argv.length < 1) { System.stdout.println("Need arguments"); return 0; } return resolve(new Number[]{ Integer.decodeString(argv[1]), Integer.decodeString(argv[2]), }).toInt(); } protected Number resolve(const Number[] &numbers) throws PolynomialException { if (numbers.length != 2) { throw new IllegalArgumentException("Only 2 numbers are expected, not: " + numbers.length); } return resolve(numbers[0], numbers[1]); } /** Solve a*x+b=0 */ public Integer polynome(const Integer &a, const Integer &b) throws PolynomialException { if (a == 0) { throw new PolynomialException("The value of a is zero"); } return -b/a; } }

docs.text: The compiler front-end will be part of this project, and LLVM will be used for the back-end . Compilation phases per compilation unit : Lexical Analyzer : Produce tokens. Syntax Analyzer : Produce an Abstract Syntax Tree by the usage of an hand written Recursive Descent Parser . Semantic Analyzer : Check languages rules. SSA Converter : Convert the AST tree into a SSA tree. LLVMifier : Convert the SSA tree into LLVM assembly language. llvm-as : Translate LLVM assembly language into LLVM bitcode. llvm-link : Link all compilation units together. opt : Run global optimization llc : Compile optimized LLVM bytecode into a native object file lld : Use the LLVM linker to link the native object file into an executable binary. 1 Optionally, the 9th and 10th step can be replaced with lli to directly execute program from LLVM bitcode.

docs.text: Meuh language syntax This document present a syntactic grammar for the language. Compilation Unit A compilation-unit define defines the overall structure of a source file. Identifier Fully Qualified Name Package Import import com.example.lib.DataType; import com.example.lib.Integer as ExampleInteger; import static com.example.lib.Integer.parseString; import static com.example.lib.Integer.parseString as exampleParseString; Struct Class Member Method Visibility
